ai_summary: Elon Musk announced a successful static fire test of Starship, SpaceX's next-generation fully reusable super heavy-lift launch vehicle. Static fire tests are critical engineering milestones that validate rocket engines and structural systems before flight, representing tangible progress toward SpaceX's goal of reducing launch costs and enabling Mars colonization. This demonstrates execution on SpaceX's core product development and the technical de-risking required to scale commercial space transportation.
embed_html:
Starship static fire successful! https://t.co/V3FaQEcWTR

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 15, 2026

text:
Congrats to the @Tesla_AI chip design team on taping out AI5!

AI6, Dojo3 & other exciting chips in work. https://t.co/hm54TdIzBx
summary: Congrats to the @Tesla_AI chip design team on taping out AI5! AI6, Dojo3 & other exciting chips in work.
ai_summary: Elon Musk announces that Tesla's AI chip design team has completed the tape-out (final design phase before manufacturing) of the AI5 chip, with AI6 and Dojo3 chips in active development. This demonstrates Tesla's vertical integration strategy in AI infrastructure and compute hardware, indicating the company is building proprietary silicon to support its AI and autonomous driving capabilities rather than relying solely on third-party chips—a critical competitive advantage in scaling AI workloads.
embed_html:
Congrats to the @Tesla_AI chip design team on taping out AI5!

AI6, Dojo3 & other exciting chips in work. pic.twitter.com/hm54TdIzBx

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 15, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk shares a link about restoring broken neural pathways, which relates to Neuralink's core mission of developing brain-computer interface technology to repair neurological damage and restore lost neural function. This represents a direct business update on Neuralink's clinical and technological progress in treating neurological conditions through implantable neural interfaces.
embed_html:
Restoring broken neural pathways https://t.co/9inlNmaAow

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 15, 2026

ai_summary: Tesla's AI chip design team has completed the tape-out (finalized design ready for manufacturing) of the AI5 chip, with AI6 and Dojo3 chips in development. Elon highlights that this will be one of the most produced AI chips ever and credits Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSMC) and Samsung as key manufacturing partners, demonstrating Tesla's strategy to build proprietary AI compute infrastructure for training and inference at scale.
embed_html:
And thank you to @TaiwanSemi_TSC and @Samsung for your support in bringing this chip to production! It will be one of most produced AI chips ever.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 15, 2026

ai_summary: Elon Musk confirms that Tesla's next-generation AI5 chip delivers approximately 5x the useful compute performance compared to the dual-SoC AI4 chip configuration, in response to a technical comparison thread. This represents a significant architectural improvement in Tesla's autonomous driving and AI inference capabilities, indicating substantial progress in the company's custom silicon strategy for competitive advantage in self-driving technology.
embed_html:
A single AI5 has ~5 times the useful compute of a dual SoC AI4

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 15, 2026

ai_summary: Tesla's AI chip design has reached tape out—a critical manufacturing milestone where the chip design is finalized and sent to production. Elon's reply emphasizes the intense execution required, noting the Tesla team worked every weekend for over 6 months to achieve this milestone, illustrating the relentless pace of hardware engineering and the significant effort required to advance Tesla's in-house AI chip capabilities for autonomous driving and compute infrastructure.
embed_html:
Every weekend without exception for over 6 months

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 15, 2026

ai_summary: Tesla's AI chip design team has completed the tape-out of their AI5 chip with support from TSMC and Samsung, positioning it to become one of the most produced AI chips globally. The chip will power Tesla's Optimus humanoid robots and supercomputer clusters, while the earlier AI4 chip is already sufficient for autonomous driving safety—demonstrating Tesla's vertical integration strategy in AI hardware development to support both robotics and autonomous vehicle initiatives.
embed_html:
Optimus and our supercomputer clusters.

AI4 is enough to achieve much better than human safety for FSD.

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) April 15, 2026
